author: Sören Lewis Müller
msg:
On my end in average rewards look good again across all ULOs (mostly 70/30)

Including $0.00 payouts:

Average (mean): $0.1404

Median: $0.17

25th percentile (Q1): $0.1225

75th percentile (Q3): $0.20

Std dev: $0.0761 (inflated by the many $0.00s)

Excluding $0.00 payouts (only wallets that actually earned something):

# non-zero payouts: 173 (so 37 were $0.00)

Mean (non-zero): $0.1704

Median (non-zero): $0.17

Q1/Q3 (non-zero): $0.15 / $0.20

Std dev (non-zero): $0.0437 (much tighter)

Interpretation: the “real” earning cluster is very tight around $0.15–$0.20, and the main distortion is simply the $0.00 group.

author: G Boot
msg:
Yes and No, if there is one primary group how will you prevent fishing of clients?? Will you accept some offering 10, 20% better rates with longer durations? 

You underestimate the power and importance of relationship, as well as the tribal dynamics that will naturally emerge. Any none official group will be exposed to that and you already had a taste here on TG for what this can mean. It could support as well as break some UNOs. 

Will leave it here as I won’t get involved but encourage those setting it up to think a few things through on the potential risks & issues that can come up and how to mitigate / prevent those and their potential impact.

author: Compost Man Unity code: XY
msg: Yes. It is being thought through. Such as the 50/50 minimum split being a requirement, and working with a smaller group of unos who understand the ToR.

author: G Boot
msg: Perfect. I prefer to go my own way and make splits in favour and support of my ULOs & personal business interests, which will vary on a case by case basis (range 25-90%)

author: Compost Man Unity code: XY

author: Val | WhiteBridge Noderunners | MNT SN.VN.UN
msg:
Static or dynamic IP should be irrelevant 

This project is set up to dynamic ranges as ULOs worldwide will often change IP once they go offline

The biggest question is the vpn/proxy firewall 
If they use a DNS blocking that could affect complete IP ranges many will have connection issues

IP limitations will also be an issue if limits are per IP instead of per license, this seems to be the case but we’ll need more data to analyze it

More tasks more requests means more work to be done helping those with multiple phones on 1 location
For telemetry it’s just terrible as the data will all be the same and therefore less profitable
